channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate heads toward confirming Kristi Noem as Trump's homeland security secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is heading towards a vote on confirming South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem as homeland security secretary, placing her at the head of a sprawling agency that will be essential to both national security and President Donald Trump's plans to squash illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian winemaker, US vets team up on a mission: showing the best of Ukraine, one glass at a time
item.description: MONTCLAIR, Va. (AP) — In a wine shop an hour outside of Washington, owner Arthur Lampros sampled a wine from a part of the world that was totally new to him, racking his brain to pin down the tastes on his tongue.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's border emergency declaration comes amid relative calm after years of major turmoil
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Long stretches of silence on a Border Patrol scanner are punctuated with updates on tracking a single migrant for hours. The radio traffic sounds like a throwback to earlier times, before the United States became the largest destination for asylum-seekers in 2017.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, GREGORY BULL and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Conservatives of color have lofty expectations for Trump's second term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Delivering his first address as a reinaugurated president, Donald Trump spoke directly to communities that had historically shunned his party.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic states weigh more support for immigrants as Trump administration cracks down
item.description: As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigration, lawmakers in some Democratic-led states are proposing new ways to resist his efforts.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' while touring disaster areas
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump surveyed disaster zones in California and North Carolina on Friday and said he was considering “getting rid of” the Federal Emergency Management Agency, offering the latest sign of how he is weighing sweeping changes to the nation's central organization for
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Trump's embattled pick for defense secretary, wins Senate confirmation
item.description: Vice President JD Vance cast a tie-breaking vote as Hegseth overcame allegations of sexual assault, public drunkenness and questions of financial mismanagement to win Senate approval.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Kristi Noem beats Senate confirmation hurdle, advancing to final vote for DHS role
item.description: Kristi Noem cleared a key procedural hurdle in the Senate on Friday, teeing up her final confirmation vote for early Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Just days into the new Trump administration, worries spike amid a show of force on immigration
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Less than a week into his presidency, the Trump administration touted deportation efforts and published new rules Friday making it easier to remove people — part of a flurry of actions to make good on campaign promises to crack down on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth confirmed to lead Pentagon after VP Vance casts tie-breaking vote
item.description: The Senate voted Friday night on the confirmation of Trump's pick to lead the Department of Defense, Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico denies access to land for US deportation flight after miscommunication, State Dept. official says
item.description: Mexico reportedly denied access to land for a U.S. military plane that was slated to return deportees to the country, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plea to President Trump: Tell Cuba to hand over terrorist killers it is harboring
item.description: Fifty years ago today, the Puerto Rican separatist group, the FALN, bombed Manhattan's historic Fraunces Tavern, but no one has ever been charged in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kern County reacts: Trump proposes voter ID mandate for California elections
item.description: Back when 17 News covered that story, we asked viewers if they thought voter ID should be required. More than 700 voted, and 92% said voter ID should be checked at the polls.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump meets with California residents, fire and law enforcement officials to see LA wildfire damage first hand
item.description: President Trump and first lady Melania Trump traveled to Southern California and met with residents to tour the damage of the Los Angeles wildfires on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio to Chinese foreign minister: Trump will put 'American people first' in US-China relations
item.description: New Secretary of State Marco Rubio told China's foreign minister on Friday that the U.S. would put the American people first and promote U.S. interests in its relationship with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Trump names Australian professional basketball team owner as New Zealand, Samoa ambassador
item.description: President Trump named Jared Novelly, Illawarra Hawks basketball team owner in Australia, as ambassador to New Zealand and the Independent State of Samoa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: St. Paul City Council President Mitra Jalali announces resignation, citing health concerns
item.description: After nearly seven years with the city, St. Paul council member and president Mitra Jalali has decided to step down. She says she is focusing on prioritizing her mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Trump leans in on targeting Russian oil revenue as he tries to fulfill pledge to end Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is emphasizing that targeting Russia's oil revenue is the best way to get Moscow to end its nearly three-year war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth will be confirmed, has 'big shoes to fill': Clark
item.description: "I think it's the president's choice. That's our system," the retired four-star general said ahead of Friday's Senate vote.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Dem governor backs ICE arresting 'criminals' despite vowing to use 'every tool' against Trump deportations
item.description: The Democratic Governor Maura Healey signaled her support of criminal illegal alien arrests, saying she supports “the apprehension of criminals in our communities"
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth confirmed as Trump's defense secretary in tie-breaking vote despite turmoil over his conduct
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Pete Hegseth as the nation’s defense secretary in a dramatic late-night vote.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs and sanctions if it does not agree to end war
item.description: It is unclear how much impact economic penalties would have on the Russian government, since they already face various sanctions imposed by the previous administration.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to probe local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams says he won't publicly criticize President Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams said Tuesday he would not criticize President Donald Trump publicly. Adams also doggedly defended his decision to attend Trump’s inauguration Monday and skip local Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday, including a top envoy to Iran during his first term and retired Gen. Mark Milley, who Trump tapped as Joint Chiefs chairman in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Republicans express optimism, state Democrat chair urges caution after inauguration
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Many Florida Republicans in the state legislature traveled to the nation’s capital for Inauguration Day events. Lawmakers like Sen. Rick Scott who works on Capitol Hill posted a video statement on X congratulating President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida officials react to Trump inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States on Monday. Here's how Florida officials are responding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama not attending Donald Trump's inauguration: officials
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The former first lady of the U.S., Michelle Obama, isn't attending President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday, according to her office. It's not clear why she's not at the swearing-in ceremony. Her husband, former President Barack Obama, is taking part in the event.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to issue 10 executive orders cracking down on border
item.description: President-elect Trump is set to sign off on 10 executive orders Monday, moving swiftly to crack down on immigration and drug cartels during his first day in office with a blitzkrieg of immigration actions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
